skill.name: template-skill
skill.description: A minimal starter template for creating a custom AI skill with frontmatter and instruction space.
skill.summary: Start a new AI skill from a small editable SKILL.md template.
skill.author: DYAI2025

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported two duplicate weak cryptography findings at SKILL.md:3. Review found only placeholder description text in YAML frontmatter, with no cryptographic algorithm, code execution, network access, or prompt injection evidence.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The duplicate static findings point to SKILL.md:3, which contains placeholder description text. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, cryptographic code, or security-sensitive behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line is plain YAML metadata text. The skill contains no executable code or cryptographic implementation.

content.value_statement: Starting a new AI skill can be inconsistent without a clear file structure. This template provides a minimal SKILL.md scaffold for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code users.
actual_capabilities: Provides a SKILL.md file with required YAML frontmatter.
Defines a placeholder skill name for quick customization.
Marks where authors should add task instructions.
Supports documentation of new workflows for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
Uses a minimal structure that is easy to review and extend.
limitations: It does not include finished workflow instructions.
It does not provide scripts, assets, or reference files.
It must be customized before it is useful to end users.
It does not validate skill quality or marketplace readiness.

use_cases.title: Draft a New Skill
use_cases.description: Create an initial SKILL.md file before writing detailed agent instructions.

use_cases.title: Standardize Skill Structure
use_cases.description: Start each small skill with consistent frontmatter and an instruction section.

use_cases.title: Teach Skill Format
use_cases.description: Show new contributors the basic parts of a simple AI skill package.

prompt_templates.title: Name the Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this template to create a skill name and one-sentence description for my workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Write Basic Instructions
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this template into a skill that helps with my workflow: [describe the workflow].

prompt_templates.title: Improve Skill Scope
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this template-based skill and make the trigger description precise and user-facing.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this completed skill for unclear instructions, missing boundaries, and marketplace readiness.

output_examples.input: Create a starter skill for release note writing.
output_examples.output: A concise skill draft with a clear name, a trigger description, and a section for release note workflow instructions.

output_examples.input: Help me make this template ready for a team workflow.
output_examples.output: A revised skill outline that defines when to use the skill, what inputs are needed, and what output format to produce.

output_examples.input: Check whether my template-based skill is complete.
output_examples.output: A short review that identifies missing instructions, vague scope, and any content that should move into references.

best_practices: Replace placeholder text before publishing the skill.
Keep the trigger description specific to real user tasks.
Add only essential instructions to the main SKILL.md file.
anti_patterns: Do not publish the template without meaningful instructions.
Do not use broad descriptions that trigger for unrelated tasks.
Do not include secrets, credentials, or private project data.

faq.question: Is this a finished skill?
faq.answer: No. It is a starter template that must be customized before practical use.

faq.question: What files does it include?
faq.answer: It includes one SKILL.md file with frontmatter and an instruction placeholder.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The template can be adapted for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Does it run commands or scripts?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed package contains no scripts, commands, or executable code.

faq.question: Why was cryptography flagged?
faq.answer: The static analyzer flagged line 3, but review found only placeholder description text.

faq.question: What should I add before publishing?
faq.answer: Add a precise description, task steps, expected inputs, and clear output guidance.
